svg.title: Net selectivity workflow and proportion tables
svg.desc: Diagram showing the net-selectivity pathway, where selectivity expansion is applied before tabulating count and number proportions, then weight proportions are derived from fitted length weights.
svg.text: Selectivity expansion applied before count/number proportions; weight proportions derived from fitted length weights.

text.tspan: Assign selectivity parameters based on net-type
Calculate selectivity expansion per specimen: 1 / S(L)
Count = summed selectivity expansion per age, length, sex, stratum, haul
Weight proportions calculated from mean length-binned weights per sex
Normalized weights per length, age, sex, stratum
Mean length-binned weights for each sex
Count = # specimen per age, length, sex, stratum
Weight proportions from empirical or fitted weights
Count = summed selectivity expansion per age, length, sex, stratum
Normalized counts per length, age, sex, stratum
Length, age, sex, haul UID, stratum
